title: TOGETHER WE CAN - 211info
text: BLACK PARENT INITIATIVE TOGETHER WE CAN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Culturally specific home visiting support services that include parenting education, early detection of developmental delays and health issues, increase children’s school readiness and school success, and prevent child abuse and neglect. 901 NE Glisan Street Suite 250 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Parents who identify African and African American with a child ages 2 months-5 years Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Home Visits: Varies Email (503) 284-8000 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 284-8000 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located on Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard at Stanton Street (2 blocks north of Knott Street). On TriMet bus line 6. Services: Home Based Parenting Education * African American Community Other Services Offered At This Location: HUMAN REPRODUCTION - SACRED ROOTS DOULA HUMAN REPRODUCTION - SACRED ROOTS LACTATION INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SAWUBONA If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. BLACK PARENT INITIATIVE
